briefdescription.para: check the compiled lib version against the include one.
Register a callback function that will be called on errors and warnings.

detaileddescription.para: If handler is NULL, the error handler will be deactivated.
If you only want to disable parser errors being printed to stderr, use xmlParserOption XML_PARSE_NOERROR.
This is the recommended way to collect errors from the parser and takes precedence over all other error reporting mechanisms. These are (in order of precedence):

briefdescription.para: Get the last error raised.

detaileddescription.para: Note that the XML parser typically doesn't stop after encountering an error and will often report multiple errors. Most of the time, the last error isn't useful. Future versions might return the first parser error instead.

simplesect.para: NULL if no error occurred or a pointer to the error

briefdescription.para: Reset the last parser error to success.

detaileddescription.para: This does not change the well-formedness status.

briefdescription.para: Handle an out-of-memory error.
If filename is empty, use the one from context input if available.

detaileddescription.para: Report an IO error to the parser context.

simplesect.para: true if the last error is catastrophic.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, xmlNode *node, xmlErrorDomain domain, xmlParserErrors code, xmlErrorLevel level, const xmlChar *str1, const xmlChar *str2, const xmlChar *str3, int int1, const char *msg, va_list ap)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtVErr

briefdescription.para: Raise a parser error.

parameterdescription.para: extra parameters for the message display

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, xmlNode *node, xmlErrorDomain domain, xmlParserErrors code, xmlErrorLevel level, const xmlChar *str1, const xmlChar *str2, const xmlChar *str3, int int1, const char *msg,...)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtErr

briefdescription.para: Raise a parser error.

parameterdescription.para: extra parameters for the message display

briefdescription.para: Get well-formedness and validation status after parsing.

detaileddescription.para: Also reports catastrophic errors which are not related to parsing like out-of-memory, I/O or other errors.

simplesect.para: a bitmask of XML_STATUS_* flags ORed together.

briefdescription.para: Handle a fatal parser error, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: violating Well-Formedness constraints

briefdescription.para: Return window into current parser data.

parameterdescription.para: maximum size of window (in) actual size of window (out)
offset of current position inside window (out)

briefdescription.para: Check whether the character is allowed by the production.

simplesect.para: 0 if not, non-zero otherwise

xrefdescription.para: This function was internal and is deprecated.

parameterdescription.para: an indicative size for the lookahead

simplesect.para: -1 as this is an error to use it.

briefdescription.para: Grow the input buffer.

simplesect.para: the number of bytes read or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Raises an error with code if the input wasn't consumed completely.
This function increase the input for the parser.

detaileddescription.para: It tries to preserve pointers to the input buffer, and keep already read data

parameterdescription.para: an indicative size for the lookahead

simplesect.para: the amount of char read, or -1 in case of error, 0 indicate the end of this entity

briefdescription.para: Shrink the input buffer.
This function removes used input for the parser.
Skip to the next char input char.
The current char value, if using UTF-8 this may actually span multiple bytes in the input buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Implement the end of line normalization:
2.11 End-of-Line Handling

Wherever an external parsed entity or the literal entity value of an internal parsed entity contains either the literal two-character sequence "#xD#xA" or a standalone literal #xD, an XML processor must pass to the application the single character #xA. This behavior can conveniently be produced by normalizing all line breaks to #xA on input, before parsing.)

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the length of the char read

simplesect.para: the current char value and its length

briefdescription.para: The current char value, if using UTF-8 this may actually span multiple bytes in the input buffer.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the beginning of the char
pointer to the length of the char read

simplesect.para: the current char value and its length

briefdescription.para: append the char value in the array

parameterdescription.para: pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the number of xmlChar written

briefdescription.para: append the char value in the array

parameterdescription.para: pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the number of xmlChar written

briefdescription.para: Installs a custom implementation to convert between character encodings.

detaileddescription.para: This bypasses legacy feature like global encoding handlers or encoding aliases.

briefdescription.para: Use encoding specified by enum to decode input data.

detaileddescription.para: This overrides the encoding found in the XML declaration.
This function can also be used to override the encoding of chunks passed to xmlParseChunk.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 otherwise
0 in case of success, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Use specified encoding to decode input data.

detaileddescription.para: This overrides the encoding found in the XML declaration.
This function can also be used to override the encoding of chunks passed to xmlParseChunk.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Use encoding handler to decode input data.

detaileddescription.para: Closes the handler on error.

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

briefdescription.para: Use encoding handler to decode input data.

parameterdescription.para: the parser context, only for error reporting

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Use encoding handler to decode input data.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used to enforce the encoding of chunks passed to xmlParseChunk.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Handle optional BOM, detect and switch to encoding.

detaileddescription.para: Assumes that there are at least four bytes in the input buffer.

briefdescription.para: Set the encoding from a declaration in the document.

detaileddescription.para: If no encoding was set yet, switch the encoding. Otherwise, only warn about encoding mismatches.
Takes ownership of 'encoding'.

simplesect.para: the encoding from the encoding declaration. This can differ from the actual encoding.
the actual used to parse the document. This can differ from the declared encoding.

briefdescription.para: Free up an input stream.
Create a new input stream structure.

simplesect.para: the new input stream or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *url, const char *publicId, const char *encoding, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtNewInputFromUrl

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input from the filesystem, the network or a user-defined resource loader.

simplesect.para: a new parser input.

briefdescription.para: Internal helper function.

simplesect.para: a new parser input.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *url, const void *mem, size_t size, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlNewInputFromMemory

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from a memory area.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
If the XML_INPUT_BUF_STATIC flag is set, the memory area must stay unchanged until parsing has finished. This can avoid temporary copies.
If the XML_INPUT_BUF_ZERO_TERMINATED flag is set, the memory area must contain a zero byte after the buffer at position size. This can avoid temporary copies.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *url, const void *mem, size_t size, const char *encoding, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtNewInputFromMemory

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from a zero-terminated string.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
If the XML_INPUT_BUF_STATIC flag is set, the string must stay unchanged until parsing has finished. This can avoid temporary copies.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *url, const char *str, const char *encoding, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtNewInputFromString

simplesect.para: a new parser input.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from a file descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
fd is closed after parsing has finished.
Supported flags are XML_INPUT_UNZIP to decompress data automatically. This feature is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *url, int fd, const char *encoding, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtNewInputFromFd

simplesect.para: a new parser input.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *url, xmlInputReadCallback ioRead, xmlInputCloseCallback ioClose, void *ioCtxt, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlNewInputFromIO

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from input callbacks and context.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
ioRead is called to read new data into a provided buffer. It must return the number of bytes written into the buffer ot a negative xmlParserErrors code on failure.
ioClose is called after parsing has finished.
ioCtxt is an opaque pointer passed to the callbacks.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *url, xmlInputReadCallback ioRead, xmlInputCloseCallback ioClose, void *ioCtxt, const char *encoding, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtNewInputFromIO

simplesect.para: a new parser input.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input for a push parser.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a new input stream structure encapsulating the input into a stream suitable for the parser.

simplesect.para: the new input stream or NULL

briefdescription.para: Create a new input stream based on an xmlEntity.

simplesect.para: the new input stream or NULL

briefdescription.para: Create a new input stream based on a memory buffer.

simplesect.para: the new input stream

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *url, const char *publicId, void *localCatalogs, int allowGlobal, char **out)
memberdef.name: xmlResolveFromCatalog

briefdescription.para: Resolves an external ID or URL against the appropriate catalog.

parameterdescription.para: the URL or system ID for the entity to load
the public ID for the entity to load (optional)

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code
NULL.

briefdescription.para: Create a new input stream based on a file or a URL.

detaileddescription.para: The flag XML_INPUT_UNZIP allows decompression.
The flag XML_INPUT_NETWORK allows network access.
The following resource loaders will be called if they were registered (in order of precedence):

listitem.para: the per-thread xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameFunc set with xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameDefault (deprecated)

the default loader which will return
the result from a matching global input callback set with xmlRegisterInputCallbacks (deprecated)
a file opened from the filesystem, with automatic detection of compressed files if support is compiled in.

parameterdescription.para: the filename to use as entity

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

briefdescription.para: Create a new input stream based on a file or an URL.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike the default external entity loader, this function doesn't use XML catalogs.

parameterdescription.para: the filename to use as entity

simplesect.para: the new input stream or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: By default we don't load external entities, yet.

parameterdescription.para: the URL or system ID for the entity to load
the public ID for the entity to load (optional)
the context in which the entity is called or NULL

simplesect.para: a new allocated xmlParserInput, or NULL.

briefdescription.para: A specific entity loader disabling network accesses, though still allowing local catalog accesses for resolution.

parameterdescription.para: the URL or system ID for the entity to load
the public ID for the entity to load
the context in which the entity is called or NULL

simplesect.para: a new allocated xmlParserInput, or NULL.

briefdescription.para: Changes the default external entity resolver function for the application.

xrefdescription.para: This is a global setting and not thread-safe. Use xmlCtxtSetResourceLoader or similar functions.

briefdescription.para: Get the default external entity resolver function for the application.

simplesect.para: the xmlExternalEntityLoader function pointer

briefdescription.para: Installs a custom callback to load documents, DTDs or external entities.

detaileddescription.para: If vctxt is NULL, the parser context will be passed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *url, const char *publicId, xmlResourceType type)
memberdef.name: xmlLoadResource

parameterdescription.para: the URL or system ID for the entity to load
the public ID for the entity to load (optional)

simplesect.para: the xmlParserInput or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: URL is a filename or URL.

detaileddescription.para: If if contains the substring "://", it is assumed to be a Legacy Extended IRI. Otherwise, it is treated as a filesystem path.
publicId is an optional XML public ID, typically from a doctype declaration. It is used for catalog lookups.

If catalog lookup is enabled (default is yes) and URL or ID are found in system or local XML catalogs, URL is replaced with the result. Then the following resource loaders will be called if they were registered (in order of precedence):

listitem.para: the resource loader set with xmlCtxtSetResourceLoader
the global external entity loader set with xmlSetExternalEntityLoader (without catalog resolution, deprecated)
the per-thread xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameFunc set with xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameDefault (deprecated)

the default loader which will return
the result from a matching global input callback set with xmlRegisterInputCallbacks (deprecated)
a file opened from the filesystem, with automatic detection of compressed files if support is compiled in.

parameterdescription.para: the URL or system ID for the entity to load
the public ID for the entity to load (optional)
the context in which the entity is called or NULL

simplesect.para: the xmlParserInput or NULL

briefdescription.para: Initialize a SAX parser context.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Initialize a parser context.

xrefdescription.para: Internal function which will be made private in a future version.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Free all the memory used by a parser context.

detaileddescription.para: However the parsed document in ctxt->myDoc is not freed.

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialize a new parser context.

simplesect.para: the xmlParserCtxt or NULL

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialize a new SAX parser context.

detaileddescription.para: If userData is NULL, the parser context will be passed as user data.

2.11.0
If you want support older versions, it's best to invoke xmlNewParserCtxt and set ctxt->sax with struct assignment.

simplesect.para: the xmlParserCtxt or NULL if memory allocation failed.
the private application data.

briefdescription.para: Set the private application data.

simplesect.para: the local catalogs.

briefdescription.para: Set the local catalogs.

simplesect.para: the dictionary.

briefdescription.para: Set the dictionary.

detaileddescription.para: This should only be done immediately after creating a parser context.

simplesect.para: the SAX handler struct. This is not a copy and must not be freed. Handlers can be updated.

briefdescription.para: Set the SAX handler struct to a copy of sax.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.
the parsed document or NULL if a fatal error occurred when parsing. The document must be freed by the caller. Resets the context's document to NULL.
1 if this is a HTML parser context, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the parser is stopped.

detaileddescription.para: The parser is stopped on fatal (non-wellformedness) errors or on user request with xmlStopParser.

simplesect.para: 1 if the parser is stopped, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check whether a DTD subset is being parsed.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by SAX callbacks.
Return values are

simplesect.para: the subset status
the validation context.

briefdescription.para: Return user data.

detaileddescription.para: Return user data of a custom SAX parser or the parser context itself if unset.

simplesect.para: the user data.

briefdescription.para: Return the current node being parsed.

detaileddescription.para: This is only useful if the default SAX callbacks which build a document tree are intercepted. This mode of operation is fragile and discouraged.
Returns the current element node, or the document node if no element was parsed yet.

simplesect.para: the current node.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const xmlChar **name, const xmlChar **systemId, const xmlChar **publicId)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtGetDocTypeDecl

briefdescription.para: Return data from the doctype declaration.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by SAX callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: system ID (URI) of the external subset (output)
public ID of the external subset (output)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if argument is invalid

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, int inputIndex, const char **filename, int *line, int *col, unsigned long *utf8BytePos)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtGetInputPosition

briefdescription.para: Return input position.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by error handlers or SAX callbacks.

Because of entities, there can be multiple inputs. Non-negative values of inputIndex (0, 1, 2, ...) select inputs starting from the outermost input. Negative values (-1, -2, ...) select inputs starting from the innermost input.
The byte position is counted in possibly decoded UTF-8 bytes, so it won't match the position in the raw input data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if arguments are invalid

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, int inputIndex, const xmlChar **startOut, int *sizeInOut, int *offsetOut)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtGetInputWindow

briefdescription.para: Return window into input data.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by error handlers or SAX callbacks. The returned pointer is only valid until the callback returns.

Because of entities, there can be multiple inputs. Non-negative values of inputIndex (0, 1, 2, ...) select inputs starting from the outermost input. Negative values (-1, -2, ...) select inputs starting from the innermost input.

parameterdescription.para: maximum size of window (in) actual size of window (out)
offset of current position inside window (out)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if arguments are invalid

briefdescription.para: Same as xmlCtxtReset.
Find the parser node info struct for a given node.

parameterdescription.para: an XML node within the tree

simplesect.para: an xmlParserNodeInfo block pointer or NULL

briefdescription.para: Initialize (set to initial state) node info sequence.
Clear (release memory and reinitialize) node info sequence.
Find the index that the info record for the given node is or should be at in a sorted sequence.

simplesect.para: a long indicating the position of the record

briefdescription.para: Insert node info record into the sorted sequence.
Set and return the previous value for enabling pedantic warnings.

xrefdescription.para: Use the modern options API with XML_PARSE_PEDANTIC.

simplesect.para: the last value for 0 for no substitution, 1 for substitution.

briefdescription.para: Has no effect.
Set and return the previous value for default entity support.

xrefdescription.para: Use the modern options API with XML_PARSE_NOENT.

simplesect.para: the last value for 0 for no substitution, 1 for substitution.

briefdescription.para: Set and return the previous value for default blanks text nodes support.

xrefdescription.para: Use the modern options API with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.

simplesect.para: the last value for 0 for no substitution, 1 for substitution.
